description: You are an automotive engineering instructor creating a suspension damping simulator. Create a GeoGebra project that models a 'Quarter Car' suspension system (mass-spring-damper) featuring sliders for mass (m: 20-80, increment 1), spring constant (k: 10000-40000, increment 100), and damping coefficient (c: 0-4000, increment 50). The project must include the displacement function y(x) = 0.1 * exp(-c/(2m) * x) * cos(sqrt(k/m - (c/(2m))^2) * x), decay envelope functions env_top(x) = 0.1 * exp(-c/(2m) * x) and env_bot(x) = -0.1 * exp(-c/(2m) * x), and a dynamic text element displaying the Damping Ratio (c / (2 * sqrt(m * k))). Set the final slider values to simulate a 'Worn Shock' scenario (m = 40, k = 25000, c = 500) and save the project as 'suspension.ggb' in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/.
